data.narrative:
Allen Control Systems, founded by Mike Wior, Steve Simoni, and Luke Allen, is a Series A defense and security company that has raised $42M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 private firms and 2 public companies operating in the same sector, including peers such as Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $42M in total raised as a base proxy and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $84M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $150M to $500M; the median of this set is $300M, reflecting the upper-quartile positioning of peers like Stark Defence at $500M and mid-range valuation of Vatn Systems at $300M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $71M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $192M across the two methods. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Allen Control Systems' relative positioning within the defense tech peer set. Key variables driving valuation within this range include customer traction, technology differentiation versus Stark Defence and Anduril Industries, and near-term revenue visibility. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively; peer comparables suggest material upside contingent on execution metrics not present in available data.
